title: Pak en bak van elkaar onderscheiden
description: A sleepy short one: pulling apart an ANS sentence about distinguishing pak from bak, and watching the verb land all the way at the end.

title: Klankleer en de eerste vertakkingen
description: Into phonology in the ANS: parsing a sentence about woordonderscheidende klanken, why Dutch feels so concise, and decoding a prepositional-phrase-first word order.

title: De Algemene Nederlandse Spraakkunst lezen
description: Trying to read the standard Dutch grammar in Dutch: a first run at de vs het as noun markers, and worden vs is for passive against active.

title: Het Diner, Day 1
description: First day in the book: de vs het, and reasoning my way through the opening two paragraphs one word at a time.

title: Starting Dutch
description: How I'm going about learning Dutch: reading Herman Koch's Het Diner against its translation and reverse engineering the language from real sentences.

title: Starting German
description: Starting German with Wolfgang Herrndorf's Tschick as the novel and Duden Die Grammatik as the reference, same method as Dutch on harder terrain.
